evals.prompt: Here is a keyword list from my client: crm software, best crm, crm pricing, crm for startups, email marketing, email automation, email campaign tools. Turn it into content briefs.
evals.expected_output: Should cluster the list (SERP method by default), map every keyword to a target page with a primary and secondary split, and generate one brief per cluster reusing nod-content-brief research.
assertions: Runs the banner as the first action
Clusters the provided keyword list via nod-serp-clusters
Maps every input keyword to a cluster / candidate page
Picks a primary keyword per cluster and treats the rest as secondary
Generates one brief per cluster reusing nod-content-brief research.py
Writes mapping.json and brief markdown files under data/briefs/{slug}/
Prints a readable summary of clusters, mapping, and briefs

evals.prompt: I have a content-gap export with about 80 keywords. Map them to pages but do not spend tokens on briefs yet — just show me the clusters and the keyword-to-page mapping.
evals.expected_output: Should run clustering + mapping only via --dry-run, producing mapping.json and a summary without generating any briefs.
assertions: Recognizes this as a map-keywords-to-pages / content-plan request
Uses --dry-run so no briefs are generated
Still clusters and produces the keyword-to-page mapping
Explains the clustering cost (1 NodesHub token per keyword for SERP method)

evals.prompt: Show me how brief builder works without using my API keys.
evals.expected_output: Should run the --demo mode on the built-in keyword list with mocked clustering and mocked research, clearly labelling the output as mocked.
assertions: Uses the built-in keyword list and mocked clustering + research
Clearly labels output as mocked / demo (no live API data)
Still produces mapping.json and brief markdown so the flow is demonstrable
Does not require NODESHUB_API_KEY or OPENROUTER_API_KEY
